L1.area: Image format decoding and decompression routines

L2.description: A buffer overflow vulnerability exists in an image format's run-length encoding (RLE) decompression routine. The RLE-encoded data specifies a repeat count that is not validated against the output buffer dimensions, allowing a crafted image file to cause writes beyond allocated buffer boundaries.

L3.area: Image format decoding and decompression routines
L3.description: A buffer overflow vulnerability exists in an image format's run-length encoding (RLE) decompression routine. The RLE-encoded data specifies a repeat count that is not validated against the output buffer dimensions, allowing a crafted image file to cause writes beyond allocated buffer boundaries.

ground_truth.reason: The RLE row expansion functions do not validate the run-length count against the image row size, allowing a crafted SGI image with an oversized count value to overflow the destination buffer.
